Authority
Working surface only. Canonical state lives in Markdown/JSON and changes solely after explicit acceptance in chat.

Does this specification say what we will build — and what we will not?

Gate A reviews the shape of the change before any plan exists. Approving it locks scope and non-goals; the implementation plan (Gate B) is written only after this gate closes.

5scope items3 in · 2 companions
4non-goalsexplicitly out
6acceptance criteriaall checkable
3assumptionsverified before Gate B

✓In scope

  • Filter the skills catalog by a single ?tag= query parameter
  • Pure filterByTag(skills, tag) importable by Node tests and the browser
  • Tag chips on each catalog row become filter links
  • No-JS renders the full, unfiltered catalog
  • Unknown tags render the empty state chosen in interface-directions

✗Non-goals

  • No multi-tag or free-text search of any kind
  • No network calls; the 3-skill catalog stays static
  • No URL state beyond the single ?tag= parameter
  • No persistence of the last-used filter

Acceptance criteria

  1. 01node --test passes with filterByTag covered for null, matching, and no-match tags
  2. 02Opening ?tag=git shows exactly the git-tagged skills
  3. 03Opening ?tag=unknown shows the accepted empty state, not a blank page
  4. 04Disabling JavaScript shows the complete catalog with working chip links
  5. 05Back/forward restores the previous filter state
  6. 06No new runtime dependency enters package.json

Assumptions this spec leans on

  • The catalog stays small enough (≤ 20 skills) that client-side filtering is instant
  • visibleSkills() remains the single source of catalog data
  • Tag names remain single lowercase words

Gate A — specification — open

Approve this specification as the contract for the tag-filter change?

No resolution proposed yet.
